items.title: Scale features before penalizing
items.avoid: Applying Ridge/Lasso directly to raw mixed-scale columns.
items.prefer: Wrap models in a pipeline with StandardScaler (fit on train only) before the regressor.
items.why: Penalties are symmetric in coefficient index only after features share comparable units.
items.example: make_pipeline(StandardScaler(), Ridge(alpha=1.0)) prevents a feature measured in cents from dominating the penalty budget.

items.title: Tune lambda on validation, not training loss
items.avoid: Picking the smallest alpha that minimizes training MSE.
items.prefer: Use RidgeCV/LassoCV or GridSearchCV with k-fold cross-validation on a held-out metric.
items.why: The regularization knob trades bias for variance; only out-of-sample error reveals the optimum.
items.example: Plot CV MSE vs log(alpha); choose the alpha at the minimum or within one standard error of the minimum.

items.title: Match penalty to the modeling goal
items.avoid: Defaulting to Lasso whenever you have many features.
items.prefer: Choose Ridge for correlated groups and stability; Lasso when explicit sparsity is required; Elastic Net when both matter.
items.why: L1 and L2 induce different coefficient geometries and selection behavior.
items.example: For 200 collinear polynomial terms, Ridge often generalizes better than Lasso picking an arbitrary subset.
